question: What is the key property of a max-heap?
options: The value of each node is greater than or equal to the values of its children.
The value of each node is less than or equal to the values of its children.
The tree is always a complete binary tree.
The root node always contains the median value.
explanation: A max-heap is defined by the property that for every node i (other than the root), the value of the parent is greater than or equal to the value of i. This ensures the maximum element is at the root.

question: What is the time complexity of the Heapsort algorithm?
options: O(n log n)
O(n²)
O(log n)
O(n)
explanation: Heapsort has a time complexity of O(n log n) for all cases (best, average, and worst). This is because building the heap takes O(n) time, and each of the n-1 extract-max operations takes O(log n) time.

question: Given a node at index i in a heap (with the root at index 1), what is the index of its left child?
options: 2i
explanation: In the standard array representation of a heap, the children of a node at index i are found at indices 2i (left child) and 2i + 1 (right child).

question: What is the primary purpose of the MAX-HEAPIFY procedure?
options: To correct a single violation of the max-heap property at a given node.
To build a max-heap from an unsorted array.
To extract the maximum element from the heap.
To insert a new element into the heap.
explanation: MAX-HEAPIFY assumes that the binary trees rooted at the left and right children of node i are max-heaps, but node i might be smaller than its children. It corrects this single violation by floating the value down the heap.

question: In the BUILD-MAX-HEAP algorithm, why does the loop start at the index of the last non-leaf node (floor(n/2)) and go backwards to the root?
options: MAX-HEAPIFY requires that the subtrees of the node being processed are already valid heaps. Starting from the bottom ensures this precondition is met.
It is more efficient to process the leaves first.
The root node must always be processed last to maintain the heap property.
It allows the algorithm to run in O(n log n) time instead of O(n).
explanation: The leaves are already trivial max-heaps. By starting at the last non-leaf node and moving up to the root, the MAX-HEAPIFY procedure is always called on a node whose left and right subtrees are already valid max-heaps, satisfying its precondition.
